description: Structured dataset documenting evidence-based harms of outdoor advertising. All sources are independent academic, governmental, or NGO — no advertising industry sources.

psychology.label: Psychology & Cognitive Impact
psychology.description: Audience attention metrics, cognitive load, behavioral influence, and orienting response data.

environment.label: Environmental Impact & Emissions
environment.description: Energy consumption, CO2 emissions, light pollution (skyglow), and ecological disruption.

urban.label: Urban Infrastructure & Spatial Analysis
urban.description: Visual pollution, public space privatization, physical obstruction, and pedestrian flow.

economy.label: Economy & Advertised Emissions
economy.description: Overconsumption, Scope 3 emissions, status anxiety, and socioeconomic inequality.

health.label: Public Health
health.description: Harmful product promotion (junk food, gambling), obesity rates, and societal health externalities.

culture.label: Culture & Heritage
culture.description: Cultural homogenization, commercialization of historical heritage, and loss of local identity.

equity.label: Spatial Equity & Demographics
equity.description: Disproportionate exposure of vulnerable communities and environmental justice.

regulation.label: Regulatory Frameworks
regulation.description: Smart city planning laws, zoning bans, digital upgrade loopholes, and self-regulation failures.

resources.label: Resource Consumption
resources.description: Grid impact, kW/h measurement, hardware lifecycle, and externalities of DOOH operation.

politics.label: Politics & Public-Private Partnerships
politics.description: Lobbying influence, infrastructure financing, and privatization of municipal assets.

safety.label: Traffic Safety
safety.description: Driver distraction studies, accident risks due to DOOH, and lighting glare hazards.

privacy.label: Privacy & Data Surveillance
privacy.description: Programmatic audience tracking, facial detection, and Wi-Fi sniffing in public spaces.

alternatives.label: Civic Alternatives & Solutions
alternatives.description: Arts funding, civic space utilization, and ad-free municipal revenue models.
